info.name: enterprise-auth-platform
info.description:
Postman collection for the enterprise-auth-platform API.

Covers: health, auth (register/verify/login/refresh/logout/sessions/password flows/switch-organization), organizations, members, invitations, RBAC roles + permissions, ABAC policies + conditions + assignments, plus authorization scenarios and negative tests.

Responses use a unified envelope: `{ success, statusCode, message, data, timestamp, correlationId }`. Token auto-binding scripts read `data.tokens.accessToken` etc. and persist them to the active environment.

Newman-safe order: the collection runs end-to-end without manual intervention. Destructive operations (logout, change-password, deletes) live in the final `Cleanup` folder. Cache-invalidating mutations are followed by a `POST /auth/refresh` so the next request gets a fresh accessToken with current permissionsVersion/attributesVersion.

Import this file together with `enterprise-auth-platform.local.postman_environment.json` and select that environment in Postman.

exec: // Collection-level pre-request — runs before every request
// For Newman end-to-end runs: default target IDs to the current user when unset

request.description: Returns service status, env, version, memory usage and downstream service probes.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Setup-side auth: register, login, refresh, forgot-password. Destructive flows (logout, change-password, reset, switch-org) live in later folders.

exec: // Mint a fresh email so re-runs don't collide on the unique constraint

request.description: Rotates the refresh token. Old token becomes single-use; reusing it triggers full family revocation.

item.name: Organizations
item.description: Org CRUD. The creator is auto-added as a Membership with the global `admin` role.

exec: pm.test('data is an array', function () { pm.expect(json.data).to.be.an('array'); });

item.name: Switch Organization
item.description: After an org exists, switch the active organization context on the current session — mints a new access token bound to that org's membership.

item.name: Sessions
item.description: Read-only session inspection. Revoking a session lives in the Cleanup folder.

exec: pm.test('data is an array', function () { pm.expect(json.data).to.be.an('array'); });

item.name: MFA
item.description:
Multi-factor authentication: TOTP enrollment, backup codes, and the login MFA challenge.

The pre-request scripts compute live TOTP codes from `mfaSecret` (captured by "POST /mfa/setup") using the bundled crypto-js, so the enroll → verify → challenge flow is fully runnable end to end.

item.name: OAuth
item.description:
OAuth2 social login and account linking (Google / GitHub / Microsoft).

NOTE: the `GET /oauth/:provider` and `:provider/callback` routes are browser-redirect flows and cannot be completed inside Postman — they 302 to the provider consent screen. Use a browser for the interactive parts. The linking/listing endpoints below are normal authenticated JSON APIs.

item.name: SSO (SAML)
item.description:
Enterprise SAML SSO: per-organization configuration CRUD plus the SP-initiated login and ACS endpoints.

NOTE: `GET /sso/:organizationSlug/login` 302-redirects to the IdP and `POST /sso/:organizationSlug/acs` is invoked by the IdP with a signed SAMLResponse — these are browser/IdP-driven and not fully runnable in Postman. The configuration endpoints require an org admin token.

body.raw:
{
  "organizationId": "{{organizationId}}",
  "providerName": "Okta",
  "entryPoint": "https://example.okta.com/app/abc/sso/saml",
  "issuer": "http://www.okta.com/exk1234",
  "certificate": "MIIDpDCCAoygAwIBAgIGAV2ka+55MA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBC... (paste IdP X.509 cert here)",
  "metadataUrl": "https://example.okta.com/app/abc/sso/saml/metadata",
  "isEnabled": true,
  "allowIdpInitiated": false,
  "ssoOnlyMode": false,
  "defaultRoleSlug": "member",
  "attributeMapping": {
    "email": "http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/emailaddress",
    "firstName": "http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/givenname",
    "lastName": "http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/surname",
    "department": "department",
    "jobTitle": "jobTitle",
    "groups": "http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/claims/Group"
  }
}

exec: // The IdP normally POSTs this as application/x-www-form-urlencoded after authenticating the user.

item.name: Members
item.description: List members + update ABAC attributes. In Newman runs, `targetUserId` defaults to the current user (so PATCH operates on self).

exec: pm.test('data is an array', function () { pm.expect(json.data).to.be.an('array'); });

request.description: Updating ABAC attributes bumps the membership's `attributesVersion`. Newman auto-refreshes immediately after.
Helper — refresh the access token after a cache-invalidating mutation so subsequent calls don't 401 with PERMISSIONS_VERSION_MISMATCH / ATTRIBUTES_VERSION_MISMATCH.
Idempotent — returns the existing row if (resource, action) already exists.

item.name: RBAC Roles
item.description: Org-scoped roles. Assigning a role to a membership bumps `permissionsVersion`; an auto-refresh follows.

request.description: Assigning a role bumps the target membership's permissionsVersion. Newman auto-refreshes after.

item.name: ABAC Policies
item.description: Create / list / update policies. Each mutation invalidates the per-org cache and bumps all members' versions — a refresh follows.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Same-department read access",
  "slug": "same-department-read-{{$timestamp}}",
  "description": "Members can read other users in the same department",
  "effect": "ALLOW",
  "resource": "users",
  "action": "read",
  "priority": 150,
  "isEnabled": true,
  "organizationId": "{{organizationId}}",
  "conditions": [
    {
      "attributeSource": "RESOURCE",
      "attributePath": "department",
      "operator": "EQUALS",
      "value": "MEMBERSHIP.department",
      "valueType": "STRING"
    }
  ]
}

item.name: ABAC Conditions
item.description: All conditions on a policy are AND-combined.

item.name: ABAC Assignments
item.description: Bind a policy to a role, user, or organization.

item.name: Invitations
item.description: Send/list invitations. Revoke + accept live in Cleanup and Token-Required Flows respectively.

item.name: Authorization Tests
item.description: Read-only checks against the live RBAC + ABAC engine. Tests that need bespoke state (suspended member, non-admin user) skip themselves in Newman runs.

item.name: RBAC permission allowed — list permissions as admin

exec: pm.test('Admin can list permissions (200)', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

item.name: RBAC permission denied — list permissions as plain user (manual)

request.description: Needs a non-admin access token in `accessToken`. Skipped in Newman because the auto-run uses an admin token.

exec: // Skip in Newman / auto-run because the active token is an admin token

request.description: Param orgId differs from JWT organizationId. TenantGuard rejects.
On the first run inside a single Newman invocation this returns 201. Run again (or in Postman manually) for the 409.

item.name: Token-Required Flows
item.description: Endpoints that consume a token only available from an email or invitation. Skipped in Newman by default — populate the matching env var (emailVerificationToken, passwordResetToken, invitationToken) and re-run to test these in Postman.

item.name: Cleanup
item.description: Destructive operations — run LAST. Tear down everything created during the run. Assertions are tolerant where the operation may legitimately fail (e.g. last-admin removal, permission in use).

request.description: Seeded permissions are referenced by system roles → 409. New permissions are deletable → 204.
In Newman the only member is the admin → expect 409 LAST_ADMIN_REMOVAL.
Soft-deletes the org. Existing tokens stay valid because org.status remains ACTIVE.
Revoking the current session is equivalent to logout. After this point, the access token is invalid — `POST /auth/logout` below will return 401.
